description: The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network administrator should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network administrator should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel in AOS-CX 10.13.1031?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Only static VXLAN is supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Only static VXLAN is supported.

description: The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for VSF?
Feature Caveat: VSF - The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what is the caveat related to ACLs in a VSF stack?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is a chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique ports for each service across the system.

description: The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.13.1031, what is the caveat related to ICMP Redirect?
Feature Caveat: ICMP Redirect - The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

description: MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.13.1031, what is the caveat related to MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

description: When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

description: Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

description: OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what OSPF caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: OSPF - OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what RADIUS caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what is the caveat related to REST?
REST supports 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for RIP/RIPng?
Feature Caveat: RIP/RIPng - RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Traffic convergence takes approximately two minutes when VSF switchover is performed. Scenario: This issue occurs when traffic is flowing through the switch using the uplink on the conductor. Performing a VSF switchover causes the standby to become the new conductor, and it takes approximately 2 minutes for traffic to resume using the uplink of the new conductor. Workaround: If the Uplink from the VSF is a LAG with members in Conductor/Standby/Member, the convergence time would be lesser and around 70 seconds.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
description: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what are the steps to perform a software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
